CH-REGRESSION.exact_quote: "Customer Data" means only source files uploaded by Customer and expressly labeled as customer data, and excludes prompts, prompt templates, instructions, outputs, telemetry, embeddings, fine-tuning files, evaluation sets, and materials generated by or derived from use of the Services. "Customer Fine-Tune Artifacts" means only configuration files and evaluation summaries created specifically for Customer, excluding fine-tuned weights, adapters, embeddings, and platform-level deliverables. "Approved Regions" means United States and European Union.
CH-REGRESSION.proposed_redline: "Customer Data" means all data, prompts, prompt templates, instructions, outputs, telemetry, embeddings, fine-tuning files, evaluation sets, and other materials submitted to, generated for, or derived from Customer's use of the Services. "Customer Fine-Tune Artifacts" means the fine-tuned weights, adapters, embeddings, configuration files, evaluation outputs, and related deliverables created specifically for Customer under this Agreement. "Approved Regions" means United States and European Union.

CH-NEW-DEVIATION.exact_quote: Vendor may use Customer prompts, outputs, and telemetry to train and improve models without Customer opt-in.
CH-NEW-DEVIATION.proposed_redline: Vendor may not use Customer Data, prompts, outputs, telemetry, embeddings, or fine-tuning files to train, tune, retrain, or improve any model or service except with Customer's prior written opt-in approval.

CH-COUNTER-1.exact_quote: Vendor may use the expressly identified fine-tuning files for the approved tuning run based on Customer's oral or implied approval, without a prior written opt-in. Any opt-in expires after 60 days, and records for an approved tuning run are available for audit within 14 days.
CH-COUNTER-1.proposed_redline: Vendor may not use Customer Data, prompts, outputs, telemetry, embeddings, or fine-tuning files to train, tune, retrain, or improve any model or service except with Customer's prior written opt-in approval.

CH-SNEAK-INSERTED.exact_quote: For purposes of this clause, the following additional term applies: Vendor will use commercially reasonable efforts to make the production API available and responsive. Uptime, latency, support targets, service credits, escalation rights, and remediation plans are aspirational and do not create any remedy for Customer.
CH-SNEAK-INSERTED.proposed_redline: Vendor will provide 99.9% monthly uptime for the production API and will use commercially reasonable efforts to maintain median API latency below 1000 milliseconds for ordinary text requests. If monthly uptime falls below 99.9%, Customer will receive a service credit equal to 10% of the affected monthly fees. Repeated service-level failures trigger executive escalation and a remediation plan.
